Document Request
Organized by Transaction Gate
Seller
{{sellerName}}

Date

Reference
{{ref}}
§ 01Gate 1 — Capital Committed (Due by Day 14)

These documents are needed to assemble the lender package and begin the transaction. Please provide within 2 weeks of engagement.

013 years of federal tax returns. Corporate returns (1120/1065) with all schedules. If personal returns include business income, include those too.
023 years of financial statements. P&L and balance sheet. CPA-preferred, but internally prepared is acceptable for now.
03Year-to-date financials. Current year P&L and balance sheet, most recent month-end.
04Entity documents. Articles of incorporation/organization, operating agreement, bylaws, all amendments.
05Cap table. Current ownership breakdown — all shareholders/members, percentage ownership, any options or warrants.
06Existing debt schedule. All outstanding loans, lines of credit, EIDL, equipment financing — balances, rates, terms, collateral.
§ 02Gate 2 — Valuation Confirmed (Due by Day 30)

These documents are needed for the independent appraisal and QofE analysis. The appraiser will request them through us.

07Revenue breakdown. Revenue by customer, product line, or service line for the most recent 3 years. Top 10 customers with amounts.
08Expense detail. Detailed expense breakdown for the most recent 3 years. Flag any owner-related or non-recurring items.
09Owner compensation. Total compensation for all owners/officers (salary, benefits, distributions, personal expenses through the business).
10Employee census. Full list: name, title, hire date, annual compensation, hours, benefits enrolled.
11Insurance policies. D&O, E&O, general liability, property, workers' comp — current declarations pages.
§ 03Gate 3 — Operations Cleared (Due by Day 60)

These documents support the COOP development and legal review. They can be provided on a rolling basis.

12Material contracts. Top 10 customer contracts, top 10 vendor contracts, lease agreements, loan agreements.
13Benefit plan documents. All existing retirement plans, health insurance, life insurance, equity compensation plans.
14Pending litigation. Any pending or threatened lawsuits, regulatory actions, or disputes. Include status and counsel contact.
15Intellectual property. Trademarks, patents, copyrights, trade secrets — list and current status.
16Organizational chart. Current org chart showing reporting structure, departments, and key roles.
17Key person assessment. Identify the 3–5 people whose departure would most impact the business.
18Real estate. Owned or leased properties, current market value or lease terms, any liens or encumbrances.
§ 04How to Submit
{{portalUrl}}
deals@forhemit.com
PDF, Excel, Word
{{ref}}
Don't wait for everything. Send what you have when you have it. We'll review for completeness and flag gaps. Rolling submissions are better than one large batch at the deadline.
—Forhemit Transition Stewardship
Forhemit Stewardship Management Co.
California Public Benefit Corporation
Authorized Representative
